items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric` **Service directory:** `sdk/servicefabric/Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric/` **Triggered by:** PR #58149 (commit `91e692166d`) by `@live1206` ## What Changed PR #58149 completed the MPG migration for `Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric`, regenerating the entire package from TypeSpec. This introduced/confirmed all core resource types (`ServiceFabricClusterResource`, `ServiceFabricApplicationResource`, `ServiceFabricApplicationTypeResource`, `ServiceFabricApplicationTypeVersionResource`, `ServiceFabricServiceResource`, `ServiceFabricVmSizeResource`) and their associated collections and extension methods. ## Gaps Found - **CHANGELOG is empty for 1.2.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)**: The TypeSpec migration should be documented ŌĆö at minimum noting the regeneration and any behavioral changes or new API surface introduced by the 2023-11-01-preview API version. - **README "Key concepts" section is generic**: It only links to the general Azure SDK docs page. There are no ServiceFabric-specific key concepts (clusters, node types, applications, application types, services, reliability/durability levels, upgrade policies, etc.). - **README "Examples" section has no inline code snippets**: It only links to a general samples gallery. There are no embedded code examples for the most common operations (create a cluster, list clusters by subscription, create an application, update a cluster). - **No handwritten sample files with `#region Snippet:` tags**: Generated samples exist in `tests/Generated/Samples/` but lack `#region Snippet:` markers and are not wired to README placeholders, so the Update-Snippets tooling cannot inject them into the README.
­¤ōÉ Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Update the CHANGELOG **File:** `sdk/servicefabric/Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric/CHANGELOG.md` **Action:** Edit ŌĆö populate the `1.2.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)` section. ```markdown ## 1.2.0-beta.3 (Unreleased) ### Features Added - Migrated SDK generation from AutoRest to TypeSpec. - Regenerated from the `2023-11-01-preview` API version specification. ``` --- ### Step 2: Update README Key Concepts section **File:** `sdk/servicefabric/Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric/README.md` **Action:** Edit ŌĆö replace the current "Key concepts" section body with ServiceFabric-specific concepts. Replace: ```markdown ## Key concepts Key concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted) ``` With: ```markdown ## Key concepts The `Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric` library provides management-plane access to Azure Service Fabric resources. The key resource hierarchy is: - **`ServiceFabricClusterResource`** ŌĆö represents a Service Fabric cluster; this is the top-level resource managed by this library. Clusters define the node types, reliability level, and durability level for the distributed system. - **`ServiceFabricApplicationTypeResource`** ŌĆö represents a named application type registered on the cluster. - **`ServiceFabricApplicationTypeVersionResource`** ŌĆö represents a specific version of an application type, including the package URL. - **`ServiceFabricApplicationResource`** ŌĆö represents a deployed instance of an application type on a cluster. - **`ServiceFabricServiceResource`** ŌĆö represents a service within a deployed application. - **`ServiceFabricVmSizeResource`** ŌĆö read-only resource exposing the supported VM sizes for Service Fabric node types in a given location. For additional general concepts about the Azure Resource Manager .NET SDK, see the [Azure SDK for .NET documentation]((azure.github.io/redacted) ``` --- ### Step 3: Add handwritten sample file with snippet regions **File:** `sdk/servicefabric/Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric/tests/Samples/Sample_ServiceFabricCluster.cs` *(create)* ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using System.Collections.Generic; using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure.Core; using Azure.Identity; using Azure.ResourceManager; using Azure.ResourceManager.Resources; using Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric; using Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric.Models; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric.Samples { public class Sample_ServiceFabricCluster { [Test] [Ignore("Only validating compilation of examples")] public async Task CreateOrUpdateCluster() { #region Snippet:CreateServiceFabricCluster TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential(); ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred); string subscriptionId = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"; string resourceGroupName = "myResourceGroup"; ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupResourceId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName); ResourceGroupResource resourceGroupResource = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupResourceId); ServiceFabricClusterCollection clusters = resourceGroupResource.GetServiceFabricClusters(); ServiceFabricClusterData data = new ServiceFabricClusterData(new AzureLocation("eastus")) { ManagementEndpoint = new Uri("(mycluster.eastus.cloudapp.azure.com/redacted)"), NodeTypes = { new ClusterNodeTypeDescription("nt1vm", 19000, 19007, true, 5) { ApplicationPorts = new ClusterEndpointRangeDescription(20000, 30000), EphemeralPorts = new ClusterEndpointRangeDescription(49000, 64000), DurabilityLevel = ClusterDurabilityLevel.Silver, } }, ReliabilityLevel = ClusterReliabilityLevel.Silver, FabricSettings = { new SettingsSectionDescription("Security", new[] { new SettingsParameterDescription("ClusterProtectionLevel", "EncryptAndSign") }) }, }; ArmOperation lro = await clusters.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, "myCluster", data); ServiceFabricClusterResource cluster = lro.Value; Console.WriteLine($"Cluster ID: {cluster.Id}"); #endregion } [Test] [Ignore("Only validating compilation of examples")] public async Task ListClustersBySubscription() { #region Snippet:ListServiceFabricClusters TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential(); ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred); SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); await foreach (ServiceFabricClusterResource cluster in subscription.GetServiceFabricClustersAsync()) { Console.WriteLine($"Cluster: {cluster.Data.Name}, Location: {cluster.Data.Location}"); } #endregion } } } ``` --- ### Step 4: Update README Examples section with snippet placeholders **File:** `sdk/servicefabric/Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric/README.md` **Action:** Edit ŌĆö replace the current "Examples" section body. Replace: ```markdown ## Examples Code samples for using the management library for .NET can be found in the following locations - [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted) ``` With: ```markdown ## Examples ### Create a Service Fabric cluster ```C# Snippet:CreateServiceFabricCluster ``` ### List all clusters in a subscription ```C# Snippet:ListServiceFabricClusters ``` For additional code samples, see the [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted) ``` > **Note:** The closing fences above are triple-backtick fences in the actual README. They are shown here inside a code block for illustration. Ensure the snippet placeholder fences use exactly `` ```C# Snippet: `` and `` ``` `` on the closing line. --- ### Step 5: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/servicefabric/Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric/README.md` already contains all required top-level sections in the correct order. The following sections need content improvements (addressed above): | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started ŌåÆ Install the package | Ō£ģ Complete | | Getting started ŌåÆ Prerequisites | Ō£ģ Complete | | Getting started ŌåÆ Authenticate the client | Ō£ģ Complete | | Key concepts | ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Generic link only ŌĆö add ServiceFabric-specific concepts (Step 2 above) | | Examples | ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Generic link only ŌĆö add inline snippets (Step 4 above) | | Troubleshooting | Ō£ģ Complete | | Next steps | Ō£ģ Complete | | Contributing | Ō£ģ Complete | --- ### Step 6: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/servicefabric/Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/servicefabric/Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 servicefabric` ŌĆö injects snippet code from the new sample file into README.md placeholders; check git diff to confirm README was updated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 servicefabric` ŌĆö regenerates API listing files (no public API changes expected, but run to confirm) 5. `dotnet format sdk/servicefabric/Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric/` ŌĆö ensures consistent code formatting
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [ServiceFabric] Docs: README examples, key concepts, and CHANGELOG missing after TypeSpec migration
